title: Require Service Spine Topology
forbids: a missing root or module anchor, a second service spine, a module-owned context or implementer, or an open-ended router interior
why: Standalone services and API-embedded service interiors share one stable oRPC spine. Closing only its semantic anchors makes alternate authority visible without requiring models, middleware, persistence, or tests that a service does not need. A module may split procedure handlers under router only through one closed index and named router files; router.ts remains the module boundary.
remediate: Keep base.ts, contract.ts, impl.ts, modules, and router.ts at the service root. Keep contract.ts, module.ts, and router.ts in every module. When a module needs a router directory, expose one index.ts and only named *.router.ts leaves, then compose that plain map through the module router.ts boundary. Add optional model, middleware, persistence, errors, tests, or Nx metadata only where the owning semantics require them.
message: Every service interior must retain one generic root spine and generic module shells.
